title: alnus glutinosa
intro: Alnus glutinosa, also known as black alder, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like salicin and flavonoids. The health benefits of Alnus glutinosa include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent cardiovascular diseases. Herbal preparations of Alnus glutinosa can be made into tea, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
